title: tilia mississippiensis
intro: Tilia mississippiensis, also known as the Mississippi basswood, is a deciduous tree native to North America, belonging to the Tilia genus of the family Malvaceae. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The medicinal properties of Tilia mississippiensis have also been used to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to make calming beverages, salves, and creams for topical application.
